---
title: "Reproducibility"
---

# Reproducibility {#sec-appendix-reproducibility}

This appendix documents the software environment, configuration parameters, data processing steps, and analysis scripts required to reproduce all results presented in this report.

## Software Environment {#sec-repro-env}

All analyses were conducted using bayesRVE version 0.6.0 running on the following platform.

**Operating system.** macOS (ARM64, Apple Silicon).

**R and core packages.** @tbl-repro-env lists the R packages and minimum versions used throughout the report.

| Package | Min. Version | Role |
|:--------|:-------------|:-----|
| R | 4.5.1 | Base language |
| bayesRVE | 0.6.0 | Bayesian RVE via CCC |
| cmdstanr | 0.8.0 | Stan backend for MCMC sampling |
| metafor [@viechtbauer2010] | 4.0.0 | Frequentist meta-analysis (REML, CHE) |
| clubSandwich | 0.5.10 | CR2 sandwich variance reference implementation |
| robumeta | --- | CE robust variance estimation [@hedges2010] |
| checkmate | 2.1.0 | Input validation |
| cli | 3.4.0 | User-facing messages and warnings |
| rlang | 1.1.0 | Non-standard evaluation infrastructure |
| Matrix | 1.5.0 | Sparse matrix operations |
| digest | 0.6.29 | Provenance fingerprinting |
| posterior | 1.4.0 | Posterior draws manipulation |
| bayesplot | 1.10.0 | MCMC diagnostics and trace plots |
| loo | 2.6.0 | Leave-one-out cross-validation |
| ggplot2 | 3.4.0 | Visualization |
| patchwork | 1.1.0 | Multi-panel figure composition |
| testthat | 3.0.0 | Unit testing framework |

: Software environment for bayesRVE analyses. {#tbl-repro-env}

**CmdStan.** All Stan models were compiled and sampled via CmdStan (development version installed from `stan-dev/cmdstanr`). Three Stan programs ship with the package:

- `bm_ce_ncp.stan` --- Correlated-effects model, non-centered parameterization (default).
- `bm_ce_cp.stan` --- Correlated-effects model, centered parameterization.
- `bm_che_ncp.stan` --- Correlated-hierarchical-effects model, non-centered parameterization.

Stan models are located in `bayesRVE/inst/stan/` and are compiled at first use via `cmdstanr::cmdstan_model()`.


## Random Seeds and MCMC Configuration {#sec-repro-seeds}

All MCMC analyses used the following configuration, set explicitly in every analysis script.

| Parameter | Value |
|:----------|:------|
| Random seed | `20260324` |
| Chains | 4 |
| Warmup iterations (per chain) | 2,000 |
| Sampling iterations (per chain) | 2,000 |
| Total posterior draws | 8,000 (4 chains $\times$ 2,000) |
| `adapt_delta` | 0.95 |
| `max_treedepth` | 12 |
| Working correlation ($\rho$) | 0.6 |

: MCMC configuration for all bayesRVE analyses. {#tbl-repro-mcmc}

Seeds are passed to `bm_fit()` via the `seed` argument, which forwards to `cmdstanr::sample()`. No implicit random number generation occurs outside of Stan sampling. The elevated `adapt_delta` (0.95, versus the Stan default of 0.80) reduces the risk of divergent transitions in hierarchical models.

**Priors.** All analyses used the following weakly informative priors:

- Regression coefficients: $\beta_k \sim \text{Normal}(0, 10)$
- Between-study SD (CE and CHE): $\tau \sim \text{half-Cauchy}(0, 0.5)$
- Within-study SD (CHE only): $\omega \sim \text{half-Cauchy}(0, 0.5)$

These priors are set via `bm_prior()` and passed to `bm_fit()`.


## Data Processing Notes {#sec-repro-data}

**Source dataset.** The empirical application uses the @tannersmith2015 dataset of brief alcohol interventions for adolescents and young adults, as reanalyzed by @pustejovsky2022. The dataset is stored as an RDS file at:

```
dev/manuscript/data/Tanner-Smith-Lipsey-2015-subset.rds
```

**Analytic subset.** Records with missing values on `percoll`, `attrition_all`, or `permale` are excluded, yielding:

- $J = 107$ studies
- $N = 1{,}198$ effect sizes

**Effect size metric.** Standardized mean difference (Hedges' $g$), with sampling variances reported in the original dataset (variable `V`).

**Imputed covariance matrix.** The block-diagonal working variance-covariance matrix is constructed via `metafor::impute_covariance_matrix()` with $\rho = 0.6$ and `smooth_vi = TRUE`.

**Moderator centering.** All continuous moderators are centered prior to model fitting. @tbl-repro-center documents the transformations applied.

| Variable | Centering |
|:---------|:----------|
| `postwks_c` | $\min(\texttt{postwks}, 26) - 12$ |
| `postwks_long` | Indicator: $\texttt{postwks} > 26$ |
| `percoll_c` | $\texttt{percoll} - 1$ |
| `permale_c` | $\texttt{permale} - 0.5$ |
| `attrition_c` | $\texttt{attrition\_all} - \text{median}(\texttt{attrition\_all})$ |

: Moderator centering transformations applied to the TSL15 dataset. {#tbl-repro-center}

**Categorical moderators.** `dv_cat` (6 outcome categories) and `Ctype` (4 control-group types) are converted to unordered factors. All models use cell-mean coding (no intercept; formula prefix `0 +`).


## Chapter-to-Script Map {#sec-repro-scripts}

@tbl-repro-scripts maps each chapter of the report to the analysis scripts and key output files that produced its results.

| Chapter | Content | Analysis Script | Key Output |
|:--------|:--------|:---------------|:-----------|
| Ch 7 | Stan model specification | `bayesRVE/inst/stan/bm_ce_ncp.stan`, `bm_ce_cp.stan`, `bm_che_ncp.stan` | Compiled Stan executables |
| Ch 10 | Validation and testing | `bayesRVE/tests/testthat/test-*.R` (39 files) | 2,701+ passing tests |
| Ch 11 | CE empirical application | `dev/log/201_real-data-v060/00_full_analysis.R` | `v060_results.rds` |
| Ch 12 | CHE empirical application | `dev/log/201_real-data-v060/00_full_analysis.R` | `v060_results.rds` |
| Ch 13 | SE ratio analysis | `dev/log/201_real-data-v060/00_full_analysis.R` | `v060_results.rds` |

: Mapping from report chapters to analysis scripts and output files. {#tbl-repro-scripts}

The primary results object `v060_results.rds` (607 MB) contains all fitted model objects, sandwich variance objects, CCC calibration objects, MCMC draws for variance components, and pre-computed summary tables. This file is generated by running `00_full_analysis.R` end-to-end with the MCMC settings documented in @tbl-repro-mcmc.

**Reproduction procedure.** To reproduce the empirical results from a clean state:

1. Install bayesRVE v0.6.0 and all dependencies listed in @tbl-repro-env.
2. Ensure CmdStan is installed and accessible via `cmdstanr::cmdstan_path()`.
3. Place the TSL15 dataset at the path specified above.
4. Execute `00_full_analysis.R`. Runtime is approximately 20--40 minutes depending on hardware (ARM64 Mac: ~25 minutes).
5. The script writes `v060_results.rds` and CSV summary tables to the output directory.


## Test Suite Summary {#sec-repro-tests}

The bayesRVE package includes a comprehensive test suite executed via `testthat` (edition 3). @tbl-repro-tests summarizes the current results as of v0.6.0.

| Metric | Value |
|:-------|:------|
| Test files | 39 |
| Total tests | 2,701 |
| Passing | 2,701 |
| Failures | 0 |
| Warnings | 81 (Stan sampling; expected) |
| Skips | 4 (clubSandwich not installed) |

: Test suite summary for bayesRVE v0.6.0. {#tbl-repro-tests}

Tests are organized into groups targeting distinct validation layers. @tbl-repro-test-groups documents the major groups.

| Test Group | Approx. Tests | What It Validates |
|:-----------|------:|:------------------|
| Gate 1 ($\omega = 0$) | 75 | CHE reduces to CE at machine precision |
| CE CR2 contract | 240 | CE sandwich object structure and invariants |
| CHE CR2 contract | 205 | CHE sandwich object structure and invariants |
| CHE CR2 edge cases | 304 | $K=1$, $K=3$, unbalanced clusters, backward compatibility |
| D25 plug-in parity | -- | REML-injected estimates match clubSandwich at $10^{-6}$ |
| Tier 0 (algebraic) | 150 | CCC matrix algebra on synthetic data (machine precision) |
| Tier 1 (unit) | 200 | Individual helper functions |
| Tier 2 (benchmark) | 100 | Live-fit SE and df within 10--15% of clubSandwich |
| Tier 3 (integration) | 50 | Full pipeline on TSL15 ($J = 107$, $N = 1{,}198$) |

: Test groups and validation targets. {#tbl-repro-test-groups}

Tests can be executed from the package root with:

```r
devtools::test("bayesRVE")
```

The D25 plug-in parity tests inject REML point estimates for $\hat\tau^2$ (and $\hat\omega^2$ for CHE) into the bayesRVE sandwich machinery and verify that the resulting $V_\text{CR2}$ matrix matches the clubSandwich reference implementation at a tolerance of $10^{-6}$. This isolates the linear algebra from MCMC sampling noise and confirms correctness of the CR2 implementation.


## Version History {#sec-repro-versions}

@tbl-repro-versions documents the development milestones of bayesRVE from initial release through the version used in this report.

| Version | Date | Key Feature | Tests |
|:--------|:-----|:------------|------:|
| v0.1.0 | 2026-03-10 | Initial release: `bm_fit()` + `sandwich()` + `ccc()` | 394 |
| v0.2.0 | 2026-03-15 | Small-sample corrections ($c_\beta$, leverage, fallback) | 394 |
| v0.3.0 | 2026-03-19 | Observation-level sandwich (within-study varying moderators) | 1,072 |
| v0.4.0 | 2026-03-22 | CHE model ($\tau^2 + \omega^2$, `bm_che_ncp.stan`) | 1,159 |
| v0.5.0 | 2026-03-23 | CE Three-Layer Calibration (CR2 + Satterthwaite) | 1,191 |
| v0.6.0 | 2026-03-25 | CHE Three-Layer Calibration + API hardening | 2,701 |

: bayesRVE version history. {#tbl-repro-versions}

All results in this report were produced with v0.6.0. The version can be verified at runtime with `packageVersion("bayesRVE")`.
